Uses of Class
com.nib.commons.storage.AbstractNNumeric

Packages that use AbstractNNumeric
Package
Description
com.nib.commons.io.vsam
Provides classes and interfaces for emulating VSAM (Virtual Storage Access Method) file organizations.
com.nib.commons.storage
Provides components for managing memory storage and simulating mainframe-like data types.
com.nib.commons.utils
General purpose utility classes for common operations.
com.nib.scripting.utils.nibsort
SORT
com.nib.supernaut.commands
CICS-like commands API classes.
com.nib.supernaut.jms.api
Supernaut JMS API
com.nib.supernaut.values
Value objects and constants for Supernaut.

  • Uses of AbstractNNumeric in com.nib.commons.io.vsam

    Methods in com.nib.commons.io.vsam with parameters of type AbstractNNumeric
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    FileIOResponse
    AbstractRecordRelativeFile.read(AbstractNNumeric rrn)
    Reads a record from the file based on the specified relative record number (RRN).
    FileIOResponse
    AbstractRecordRelativeFile.read(AbstractNNumeric rrn, boolean equal)
    Reads a record from the file based on the specified relative record number (RRN) and an equality condition, with no update applied during the operation.
    FileIOResponse
    AbstractRecordRelativeFile.read(AbstractNNumeric rrn, boolean equal, boolean update)
    Reads a file record based on the specified relative record number (RRN), equality condition, and update flag.
    AbstractRecordRelativeFile
    AbstractRecordRelativeFile.relativeKeyIs(AbstractNNumeric relativeKey)
    Sets the RRN key for the file and returns the current instance.
    RecordRelativeFile
    RecordRelativeFile.relativeKeyIs(AbstractNNumeric relativeKey)
    Sets the relative key field for the current instance of RecordRelativeFile and returns the instance itself.

  • Uses of AbstractNNumeric in com.nib.commons.storage

    Subclasses of AbstractNNumeric in com.nib.commons.storage
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    AbstractNDecimal
    AbstractNDecimal is an abstract class designed to handle numeric values with a specified number of significant digits and adjustable decimal precision.
    class 
    AbstractNFloatingPoint
    AbstractNFloatingPoint is an abstract class representing a floating-point numeric data type.
    class 
    NDouble
    NDouble is a concrete implementation of the AbstractNFloatingPoint class, representing a double-precision floating-point numeric data type.
    class 
    NFloat
    The NFloat class represents a single-precision floating-point numeric type.
    class 
    NInt16
    Represents a signed 16-bit integer (2-byte) data type.
    class 
    NInt32
    Represents a 32-bit signed integer within the numeric data types hierarchy.
    class 
    NInt64
    The NInt64 class represents a 64-bit signed integer data type.
    class 
    NInteger
    The NInteger class provides an implementation for handling numeric integer variables.
    class 
    NPacked
    The NPacked class represents a packed decimal data type typically used in memory-based numerical operations.
    class 
    NPointer
    Persistent pointer to a memory location.
    class 
    NUInt16
    The NUInt16 class represents an unsigned 16-bit integer.
    class 
    NUInt32
    The NUInt32 class represents a 32-bit unsigned integer in a specific data area structure.
    class 
    NZoned
    Represents a numeric, zoned-decimal formatted field with support for variable length, decimal places, and optional signed values.

    Method
    Description
    AbstractNNumeric
    AbstractNNumeric.add(long value)
    Adds a long value to this numeric field.

    Adds the value of an NInteger to this numeric field.
    AbstractNNumeric

    Adds a BigDecimal value to this numeric field.

    Checks if this numeric field is greater than or equal to another AbstractNNumeric.

    NIndex.greaterEqualThan(AbstractNNumeric value)
    Compares the current object's numeric value with the specified value to determine if it is greater than or equal to the given value.

    Checks if this numeric field is greater than another AbstractNNumeric.
    boolean
    NIndex.greaterThan(AbstractNNumeric value)
    Compares the integer value of this NIndex instance with the specified AbstractNNumeric value to determine if the current value is greater.

    Checks if this numeric field is less than or equal to another AbstractNNumeric.

    Compares the current object's numeric value with the given numeric value to determine if it is less than or equal to the provided value.

    Checks if this numeric field is less than another AbstractNNumeric.
    boolean

    Compares the current object's numerical value with the given value to determine if it is less.
